name: Mitral Valve Disease (MVD)
description: MVD is the most common heart disease in dogs, characterised by progressive degeneration of the mitral valve leaflets causing regurgitation of blood back into the left atrium. It leads to cardiac enlargement and eventually congestive heart failure.
treatment_overview: The EPIC and PROTECT trials established that Pimobendan delays onset of heart failure in preclinical stages. Once failure develops, diuretics, ACE inhibitors, and Pimobendan are combined.
prevention_tips: Follow Cavalier Health cardiac breeding protocol
Breed only from clear parents over recommended age
